programmes.show_description: Has punk poet John Cooper Clarke got the slippery quality needed to excel on the devious comedy panel show? Greg Davies, Alex Jones and Rick Edwards also join the regulars.
Kit and Kaboodle: Stephen Fry covers the whole kit and kaboodle of K-themed topics, from kitty litter to Kitty Fisher! With Noel Fielding, Ross Noble, Colin Lane and Alan Davies.
As mankind continues to hit snooze on the Doomsday Clock, Frankie Boyle helps make sense of things with the help of Sara Pascoe, Katherine Ryan, Mona Chalabi and special guests.
Singles Week: More tender loving humour from the backwater town. Singles Week kicks off just as Jocelyn's baby is born, and Patrick and David take their relationship to the next level.
Merry Christmas, Johnny Rose: The fourth series finale of the hit sitcom. Johnny is hoping to ring in the holidays with a traditional Rose Christmas party, but it won't be easy...
The Crowening: The first episode in the fifth series of the hit sitcom. Moira is in Bosnia in a bid to revive her acting career, and David attempts to enhance his relationship with Patrick.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Breakfast: The Hairy Bikers look for great ways to kick start the day. They find out about how our morning meal has changed and evolved through the years.
Follow Lancashire Police's Major Investigation Team as they run a murder case where forensics, digital data, and good old-fashioned police work help solve a brutal killing.
Raw footage and powerful police testimony reveals the tragic reality of policing our roads and a ground-breaking investigation into the Body in the River murder.
One Day Mission: A freight train needs an urgent engine replacement after a critical failure, and a crew works on a complex bogie change for a 132 T locomotive.

Guadalajara to Puerto Vallarta: Leaving the Baja Peninsula, Rick explores Mexico's western mainland and visits the town that put tequila - and the country itself - on the world map: Guadalajara. S1 Ep
Officers at Eagle Pass intercept a driver smuggling narcotics over the border, and a group of passengers in Laredo refuse to admit to owning an air compressor full of rifles.
Officers in Progreso detain a man who made a dash back toward Mexico, and the team in Laredo uncovers methamphetamine concealed in a shipment of landscaping stone.
Hydroplaning Bike: The motorbike racer and mechanic heads to Bala Lake to try to set the world record for riding a motorcycle on the surface of water.
Motoring mayhem. The boys race three budget convertibles - a McLaren 12C Spider, a Ferrari 458 Spider, and an Audi R8 V10 Spyder - across Spain. Benedict Cumberbatch guest stars.
Buckle up for more four-wheeled fun. The boys try to solve Britain's flood problems by building a car that moonlights as a hovercraft. Movie star Hugh Jackman goes for a spin.
Feel Good Food: Uplifting stuff as the bikers explore how certain recipes can put a spring back in your step, whether it's a bowl of chicken noodle soup or a fish dish.
Mexico City to Oaxaca: Rick visits Mexico City to sample the Aztec cuisine, and then heads to the city of Puebla, which is the home of Mexico's famous meat stew mole.
Host Rob Brydon joins David Mitchell and Lee Mack - plus guests Larry Lamb, Jo Brand and Russell Howard - for the comedy panel show that tries to discern fact from fiction.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Our survey says... that former Family Fortunes host Les Dennis is joining actor Charlie Brooks, comedian Darren Harriott, and TV presenter Melinda Messenger on this edition of the show.
Kit and Kaboodle: Stephen Fry covers the whole kit and kaboodle of K-themed topics, from kitty litter to Kitty Fisher! With Noel Fielding, Ross Noble, Colin Lane and Alan Davies.
New. Frankie tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet with the help of a variety of guests that include comics and experts.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Stephen Mangan is guest host with comedians Ria Lina and Ross Noble joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as guest panellists to delve into the news.
Dara O Briain and co ham it up with James Acaster, Gary Delaney, Zoe Lyons and Josh Widdicombe as they ridicule what's hogging the headlines. Yes, Piggate is in the news!
Germaine Greer and Jermaine Jenas - together at last. Pointless co-host Richard Osman and QI's Alan Davies also join the hit fact-or-fiction panel show. Rob Brydon hosts.
K-Folk: Mind-blowing miscellany with Stephen Fry. Katherine Ryan, Josh Widdicombe, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies tackle tricky teasers on Korea, Kiev, the Korowai and St Kilda.
Frankie tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet with the help of a variety of guests that include comics and experts.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alexander Armstrong is guest host as panellists Mishal Husain and Geoff Norcott join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Nation of Gardeners: The bikers celebrate British food. They head into the garden, where through good times and bad our skills have inspired amazing dishes.
When Lancashire cops get a call about a crypto heist worth a staggering 100 million pounds, detectives unravel a bizarre bitcoin conspiracy by a bunch of bungling amateurs.
Gwent Police tackle the deadly impact of drugs on their community by taking down a violent gang in Newport and hunting down an addicted armed robber.
Commuter Train Challenge: A busy commuter train undergoes a tough traction motor replacement, while a leaky torque seal sidelines a sugarcane locomotive urgently needed for the harvest.
Mexico City to Oaxaca: Rick visits Mexico City to sample the Aztec cuisine, and then heads to the city of Puebla, which is the home of Mexico's famous meat stew mole.
A man wanted for serious sexual offences is apprehended in Progreso, and agents intercept a group of migrants moments after they cross the Rio Grande river.
Officers in Hidalgo seize over 200 kilos of methamphetamine concealed in a car, and the hunt is on in Detroit for invasive insects which could destroy the US timber industry.
Human-Powered Aircraft: The motoring fan is on a mission to do the seemingly impossible - fly using muscle power alone and build the world's fastest human-powered aircraft.
You don't wanna miss a thing in this edition, as Aerosmith's Steven Tyler is the rock star in a Reasonably Priced Car. The boys take a closer look at the 'crossover' hatchback.
A chest-thumping, patriotic edition of the motoring show! Those three British bulldogs toast all that is great about our motor industry and take to the country's finest roads.
Weekend Treats: The Bikers show how to turn the weekend into a gourmet extravaganza without breaking the bank as they share some money-saving secrets.
Oaxaca to Chinantlan Mountains: Rick explores the hidden land of Mexico's culinary capital Oaxaca, where you can smell the tropical fruit and Mexico's national cheese of queso oaxacana.
Rob Brydon hosts the compelling comedy panel show. Regulars David Mitchell and Lee Mack tell cock and bull stories with guests Ken Livingstone, Reginald D Hunter and Stephen Mangan.
Richard is joined by comedian Maisie Adam, satirical impressionist Rory Bremner, Olympic gold medal-winning rower James Cracknell, and actor Michelle Gayle on this edition of the show.
Our survey says... that former Family Fortunes host Les Dennis is joining actor Charlie Brooks, comedian Darren Harriott, and TV presenter Melinda Messenger on this edition of the show.
K-Folk: Mind-blowing miscellany with Stephen Fry. Katherine Ryan, Josh Widdicombe, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies tackle tricky teasers on Korea, Kiev, the Korowai and St Kilda.
New. Frankie returns to help make sense of the bewildering times we live in through stand-up, reviews and discussion as he tackles the biggest issues troubling the planet.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alexander Armstrong is guest host as panellists Mishal Husain and Geoff Norcott join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
UKIP and Mr Corbyn are the easy targets for satirical sharpshooters Dara O Briain, Hugh Dennis, Andy Parsons, James Acaster, Rob Beckett, Ellie Taylor and Josh Widdicombe.
